description: Request for resolution SLA extension for INC-2025-605 (P1 suspected model poisoning). Standard P1 resolution target is 24 hours; requesting extension to 72 hours.
justification: Investigation of suspected model poisoning requires comprehensive training data audit, model weight comparison against known-good checkpoints, and customer FinServ-Alpha model integrity verification. These steps are computationally intensive (model weight diff requires ~8 hours of GPU time) and require customer coordination for model validation. Containment was achieved within 1.25 hours — the affected training pipeline was halted and the model reverted to last known-good checkpoint. The extension is for thorough root cause analysis, not delayed containment.
compensating_controls: 1. Model reverted to last known-good checkpoint within 1.25 hours. 2. Training pipeline halted — no additional corrupted data can enter. 3. Customer FinServ-Alpha notified at 09:30 UTC on Dec 1 and approved extended investigation timeline. 4. Daily status updates provided to customer. 5. No customer-facing model predictions affected — inference runs on verified checkpoint.
review_notes: Valid extension. Investigation confirmed training data pipeline corruption (not malicious poisoning). Resolution at 60 hours — within extended 72-hour window. Customer satisfied with investigation thoroughness. Root cause: missing data validation step in training pipeline allowed corrupted CSV records from data provider.

description: Request for 10-day extension on post-mortem for INC-2025-584 due to external forensic analysis dependency.
justification: External forensic firm (Mandiant) engaged for analysis. Mandiant report expected August 25.
compensating_controls: Preliminary findings shared with leadership. All containment actions complete.
review_notes: Post-mortem delivered August 28. External forensics confirmed APT activity. Mandiant engagement was appropriate.
